title: A Loyalist's Campaign Chest
description: Open the party's purse to a favored son standing for office. Money is not virtue — but it buys the wine that buys the vote.

title: Hymns in the Old Tongue
description: Pay the choruses and the temple criers to sing the founders' virtues through the wards before the next chamber sits. The waverers will remember.

title: Buy a Farmer's Son a Seat
description: Find a steady young landowner and put the price of the Three Hundred in his hand. The machine grows by one loyal voice.

title: A Loyalty Inquiry
description: Summon the members whose favor has thinned and remind them whose patronage seated them. Pressure is its own kind of dues.

title: The Founders' Feast
description: Throw the party's own festival in the founders' honor — couches, lamb, and a procession in Palaioi colors. The old families are reminded who keeps the old ways.

title: An Almshouse for the Chora
description: Endow a hostel for the farm poor in the party's name. Charity binds the countryside to the Palaioi cause — and to its god.
